questions.question: A router is configured to allow up to 100 packets to pass through per second. If 150 packets pass through in a second, what will happen?
questions.explanation: The router will drop packets because it has reached its packet limit.

questions.question: A packet is fragmented into three segments and transmitted over a satellite link. If the link has a maximum transmission unit (MTU) of 1500 bytes, what will happen to the last fragment?
options: A) The last fragment will be dropped
B) The last fragment will be reassembled
C) The last fragment will be duplicated
D) The last fragment will be forwarded
questions.explanation: The last fragment will be dropped because the link does not have enough bandwidth to transmit it.

questions.question: A switch is configured to filter out packets destined for a specific host. What will happen to these packets?
questions.explanation: The packets will be dropped because they are not intended for the host that the switch is filtering.

questions.question: A network interface card (NIC) has a receive buffer that can hold 1000 packets. If 1500 packets are received in a second, what will happen?
questions.explanation: The packets will be dropped because the receive buffer is full and cannot hold any more packets.

questions.question: A firewall is configured to allow only packets from a specific subnet. What will happen to packets from other subnets?
questions.explanation: The packets will be dropped because they are not from the allowed subnet.
